data.partial_message: Most landmarks were clear. One direction word was missed.
data.fail_message: Make every landmark word very clear: straight / left / traffic lights / third / right / blue door.

data.pass_message: Good relay — all 5 elements covered with the most critical information first.
data.fail_message: Cover: what happened, where, how many affected, injuries, and hazards — most important first.

data.pass_message: All three sections covered clearly — the bystanders could act on your instructions.
data.partial_message: Two sections were clear. The action section was missing or vague.
data.fail_message: Tell people: what happened, who is affected, and exactly what to do NOW.

data.pass_message: Good — name, request, and number were all clear.
data.fail_message: Make sure your name and number are clearly audible — pace the digits.

data.pass_message: Clear transfer request — department name and polite tone both correct.
data.fail_message: Make the department name very clear — that is the key information.

data.pass_message: Good — name, original date, and two alternatives were all clear.
data.fail_message: Include all three: your name, the original date, and two alternative dates.

data.pass_message: All letters correctly spelled using NATO alphabet in the right sequence.
data.partial_message: Most letters correct. One NATO term was wrong or a letter was out of sequence.
data.fail_message: Learn the NATO alphabet — Alpha/Bravo/Charlie/Delta etc. Then say your surname letter by letter.

data.pass_message: Good — composure maintained and information repeated clearly after transfer.
data.fail_message: Stay calm and repeat your key information clearly after being transferred.

data.pass_message: Structured formal complaint — reference, issue, and resolution all present with the right tone.
data.partial_message: Two elements were covered. The third was missing or unclear.
data.fail_message: Cover all three: your reference number, the issue clearly, and exactly what you want done about it.

data.pass_message: Good — polite opener and location name were clear.
data.fail_message: Start with 'Excuse me' and make the location name very clear.

data.pass_message: Clear and polite — the specific item was unmistakably audible.
data.fail_message: Make the item name very clear — that is the key piece of information.

data.pass_message: Good — problem, request, and thanks were all clear.
data.fail_message: Include all three: what the problem is, what you need, and thank them.

data.pass_message: Symptom, duration, and referral request were all clear.
data.partial_message: Two elements were clear. Make sure to explicitly ask for the referral.
data.fail_message: Describe your symptom, say how long you have had it, and explicitly ask for a referral.

data.pass_message: Professional and confident — disclosure and request both clear.
data.fail_message: State your need professionally and make a specific request — no excessive apologising.

data.pass_message: Polite, clear, and assertive — interruption and restatement both done well.
data.partial_message: One element was missing — either the polite opener or the clear restatement.
data.fail_message: Start with 'Could I just clarify...' then state your original point clearly.

data.pass_message: Good — same meaning expressed in different words.
data.fail_message: Use completely different words but keep the same meaning.

data.pass_message: Meaning preserved and register raised — more professional sounding.
data.fail_message: Use more formal words: 'scheduled to commence' instead of 'starts'.

data.pass_message: Good — everyday language, same meaning.
data.fail_message: Remove the technical terms — say it as you would to a non-technical friend.

data.pass_message: Professional register — suitable for a formal workplace request.
data.fail_message: Replace 'boss' with 'manager', 'pay' with 'salary' or 'remuneration'.

data.pass_message: Good — three clearly different registers for three different audiences.
data.fail_message: Each version should use vocabulary appropriate for that specific audience.

data.pass_message: Progressive simplification achieved — third version would be clear to anyone.
data.partial_message: Simplification happened for two versions but the third was still complex.
data.fail_message: Each version must be simpler. By version 3 it should be something anyone could understand immediately.

data.pass_message: Good — 3+ observations in 20 seconds.
data.fail_message: Mention at least 3 specific things you can picture.

data.pass_message: Season and two clear reasons — all in 20 seconds.
data.partial_message: Season was stated but only one reason was given.
data.fail_message: State your season and give two clear reasons why you like it — in 20 seconds.

data.pass_message: Good — point, reason, and example all present in 45 seconds.
data.fail_message: Structure it: make a point, give a reason, give an example.

data.pass_message: Well-sequenced — 3 or more connective words and logical order throughout.
data.partial_message: Steps were in order but sequencing words were underused. Add first/then/finally.
data.fail_message: Use connecting words: 'First... then... after that... finally...' to organise your steps.

data.pass_message: Good — title, 3 key points, and conclusion all present in 90 seconds.
data.fail_message: Structure it: title → 3 key points → conclusion.

data.pass_message: Structured presentation and direct Q&A response — professional throughout.
data.partial_message: Presentation was good but Q&A response was slightly vague.
data.fail_message: Structure your solution clearly and answer the Q&A question directly and specifically.

data.pass_message: Good — 3 clear steps with connecting words.
data.fail_message: Say: First... Then... Finally... for each step.

data.pass_message: Three practical steps in logical order — clear problem-solving.
data.partial_message: Two steps were given. Add one more practical action.
data.fail_message: Think through at least 3 practical steps: what is the first thing to do, then what, then what.

data.pass_message: Good — 5+ steps in the correct sequence.
data.fail_message: You need at least 5 steps — think through the complete process from start to finish.

data.pass_message: Four ethical steps with clear reasoning — professional decision-making.
data.partial_message: Steps were ethical but one step was missing.
data.fail_message: Think through 4 steps: check the wallet, decide what to do, act on it, follow up.

data.pass_message: Good — most urgent step first, calm and decisive.
data.fail_message: Start with the most urgent action. Stay calm and prioritise.

data.pass_message: Four diplomatic steps — professional conflict resolution approach.
data.partial_message: Steps were diplomatic but one was missing.
data.fail_message: Give 4 steps: acknowledge the issue, listen to both sides, find common ground, agree next steps.

data.pass_message: Good — clear position and reason in one sentence.
data.fail_message: State yes or no and give one clear reason.

data.pass_message: Clear position and a logical supporting reason.
data.partial_message: Position was clear but the reason was vague.
data.fail_message: State agree or disagree clearly, then give your reason.

data.pass_message: Good — 3 signposted reasons in 60 seconds.
data.fail_message: Give 3 reasons using firstly/secondly/finally.

data.pass_message: Both sides presented equally with a clear transition between them.
data.partial_message: One side was longer than the other. Balance them more evenly.
data.fail_message: Spend 30 seconds on each side and use 'on the other hand' or 'however' to transition.

data.pass_message: Good — structured 90-second argument and direct 30-second rebuttal.
data.fail_message: Make your argument, listen to the counter-argument, then directly address it.

data.pass_message: Sustained, structured, and evidence-based argument for 2 minutes.
data.partial_message: Good start but the argument weakened after the first minute.
data.fail_message: Give at least 2 distinct counter-arguments with examples or evidence to support each.

data.pass_message: Good — activity and reason clearly expressed in past tense.
data.fail_message: Describe one activity you did today and say why it was good or notable.

data.pass_message: Both halves complete — best part and because clause both clear.
data.fail_message: Complete the full sentence: 'The best part of my day was [X] because [reason].'

data.pass_message: Good — activity, feeling, and anticipation all covered in 60 seconds.
data.fail_message: Cover all three: what you did, how you felt, and something you are looking forward to.

data.pass_message: All three elements clear — who, what was discussed, and what happened.
data.partial_message: Two elements were clear. One was missing.
data.fail_message: Include who you spoke to, what it was about, and what the outcome was.

data.pass_message: Good — all 4 reflective diary sections present in 2 minutes.
data.fail_message: Cover all four: events, feelings, reflections, and plans for tomorrow.

data.pass_message: Diary and self-evaluation both done — strength and improvement area were specific.
data.partial_message: Diary was good but the evaluation was vague. Be specific about what you did well and what to work on.
data.fail_message: After the diary, listen back and say one specific thing you did well and one specific thing to improve.
